english: taking place of relapses, the prevention of them, Lt.; p'yir zlóg-pa (phyir zlog pa), to bring back, to draw off, to divert from; p'yir sós-par gyúr-ba (phyir sos par gyur ba), to return to life; p'yir sáṅs-nas (phyir sangs nas), having come to himself again, having recovered, Dzl.; p'yir má-la smrás-pa (phyir ma la smras pa), he replied to his mother, Dzl.; p'yir-lóg skyón-pa (phyir log skyon pa), to make one ride backward, with the face to the horse's tail. — 2. postp. e.g. behind, after, ṅai p'yir e' gro (ngai phyir e 'gro) Pth., will you follow me? come with me? instead of this more carelessly: ṅa p'yir (nga phyir) Mil.; p'yir-bźin (phyir bzhin) = p'yi-bźin (phyi bzhin) frq. — II. afterwards, hereafter, at a later time Thgy.; p'yir óṅ-ba (phyir ong ba), to come too late Dzl. III. out, p'yir-la (phyir la) out (motion from an interior to an exterior place), p'yir tón-pa (phyir ton pa), °gró-ba ('gro ba), °dóṅ-ba ('dong ba), gśégs-pa (gshegs pa) to go out, skyúr-ba (skyur ba), to cast out, p'yir bstán-nas (phyir bstan nas), turned inside out (the lining of a coat) Glr.; p'yir °búd-pa ('bud pa) Sch.: 'to put out, to remove; to come to an end, to be completely exhausted'; sgo p'yir mi gtóṅ-ba (sgo phyir mi gtong ba), not to let out at the door, to keep locked in or shut up Pth. In C. also p'yi-la (phyi la) is used in this sense. — p'yir-źiṅ (phyir zhing) acc. to Lexx. = भूयस् more (exceeding in number or degree). IV. postp. c.g., also p'yir-du (phyir du), more rarely p'yir-na (phyir na) (W. *p'i-la* (*pi la*)) on account of, 1. (propter) = by or through, čii p'yir kyod di-ltar gyur (cii phyir khyod di ltar gyur), whereby or through what have you got into this plight? Dzl.; without kyod (khyod): where does that come from? Dzl.; *i nad či p'i-la yoṅs* (*i nad ci pi la yongs*), by what has this disease been caused? W.; gnód-pai p'yir-du (gnod pai phyir du), because I have done you harm Mil. 2. for, for the sake of (causa), for the good or benefit of, from love to Dzl.; for the purpose of, brtág-pai p'yir-du (brtag pai phyir du), in order to try or to prove Glr. Whether p'yir (phyir) with the infinitive, esp. of one-rooted verbs, is to be resolved by because or in order that, can be determined only by the context.

I. adv. behind, p'yis ni sgra °byuṅ (phyis ni sgra 'byung), behind, i.e. behind your back, voices are heard; gen. with respect to time: afterwards, later, p'yis byúṅ-ba (phyis byung ba), to arise, to follow, to come later Wdn.; also in reference to things past, of a later date than others that had happened before them Glr.; p'yis-nas kyaṅ (phyis nas kyang), also in future, in after times Mil.; p'yis-ñin (phyis nyin), on the following day (= saṅ (sang)) Dzl.; at some future time, some (future) day, Dzl.; da p'yis (da phyis) = da p'yin-čád (da phyin chad) Glr.; dus p'yis (dus phyis) = dus p'yi źig-na (dus phyi zhig na), subsequently, hereafter Pth.; p'yis skye-ba-méd-pa (phyis skye ba med pa), one that in future will not be re-born Mil.; on the other hand: p'yis skyes bu (phyis skyes bu) Sch., a son born after the death of his father; sú-bas kyaṅ p'yis (su bas kyang phyis) last of all Dzl.; p'yis-pa (phyis pa) v. p'yi-ba (phyi ba) (I.); it is also construed like a sbst.: ... tob-pai p'yis źig-na (... thob pai phyis zhig na), at a time subsequent to his having obtained, = after he had obtained Tar. — II. sbst. in compounds: clout, rag, duster, cloth, sná-p'yis (sna phyis), lág-p'yis (lag phyis), p'yág-p'yis (phyag phyis); p'yis-pa (phyis pa), v. p'yi-ba (phyi ba) II.

adj. rich, also fig.: yón-tan du-mai dpál-gyis p'yúg-par śog (yon tan du mai dpal gyis phyug par shog), may I grow rich in the splendour of numerous accomplishments! p'yúg-po (phyug po), adj. rich, sbst. a rich man, p'yúg-po čén-po źig (phyug po chen po zhig) a rich nobleman Mil.; p'yúg-mo (phyug mo) a rich lady; p'yug-kyád (phyug khyad) riches, wealth, opulence Dzl.; p'yúg-par gyúr-ba (phyug par gyur ba) to grow rich, byéd-pa (byed pa) to make rich; p'yug-dbúl (phyug dbul) rich and poor; p'yug dbul med (phyug dbul med) no difference between rich and poor Dzl.

cattle, sgoi p'yugs (sgoi phyugs) v. sgo (sgo); p'yugs °tsó-ba (phyugs 'tsho ba) to tend cattle Glr.; p'yugs-kyi śiṅ-rta (phyugs kyi shing rta) Cs., a bullock cart; p'yúgs-nad (phyugs nad) disease of cattle, murrain; nor-p'yúgs (nor phyugs), chattels, all kinds of property Dzl.
Sch. hay-rick, shock of sheaves, heap of sticks (Schr. p'yúr-ba (phyur ba), to heap up).

W. *p'e* (*pe*), resp. gsáṅ-p'ye (gsang phye), źib (zhib), 1. flour, meal, esp. 2. flour of parched barley, = rtsám-pa (rtsam pa). — 3. for p'yé-ma (phye ma), dust, powder etc.; p'ye °tág-pa (phye 'thag pa), tság-pa (tshag pa), to grind corn to flour; to sieve; p'yer °tág-pa (phyer 'thag pa), to reduce to flour. — 4. v. °byéd-pa ('byed pa). — rgyágs-p'ye (rgyags phye) flour as provision for a journey Glr.; *ñán-p'e* (*nyan pe*) W. = rtsám-pa (rtsam pa); also parched meal. lčágs-p'ye (lcags phye) iron filings; rdó-p'ye (rdo phye), stone reduced to powder, small particles of stone; spós-p'ye (spos phye), tsándan-gyi p'yé-ma (tsandan gyi phye ma), sandlewood powder, fumigating
